name: Archal
long_description:
Archal is the staging environment for AI agents. Agents can now write to databases, trigger payments, and push code to production, but dev teams today have no reliable way to test them. We created Archal because we as developers found it frustrating that only way to know what an agent would do in production is to put it in production, meaning you can only discover failures after the damage is done. 

Archal solves this gap by creating stateful clones of software services at scale, so agents can be tested against realistic environments before deployment. These clones carry the business logic and edge cases that make them indistinguishable from the real services to AI agents, meaning agents and harnesses can be tested before they enter production.

Our platform goes beyond agents by being the ideal platform for testing any outward interacting software. Any code that creates tickets, sends messages, implements business logic should be tested, but mocks are insufficient. Our simulation of software worlds solves this problem, changing the nature of what can feasibly be tested by developers.

You would never hire an employee without interviewing them or force push code to the real world. Why would you do the equivalent for an agent?
one_liner: The eval platform for autonomous software

name: Tsenta
long_description:
Tsenta is a desktop app that matches you with jobs, optimizes your profile & applies on your behalf. It runs on your device, so you have granular control over every application being submitted in real-time.

We built Tsenta after applying to over 3,000 jobs manually, so that nobody else would have to.

Check us out at https://tsenta.com :)
one_liner: We match you with jobs and apply automatically
